bullets: Thirty-six known past market events, including crises, bullish catalysts, and narrative reversals, form the evaluation set; period and geography not stated.
Four LLM agents playing bull, bear, skeptic, and editor roles debate each narrative, and Claude, GPT-4o, and Gemini scores are ensembled into a conviction measure benchmarked against FinBERT.
The framework flags 86 percent of events before they occur versus FinBERT's 67 percent, and finds structural fragility in 10 events where FinBERT reads neutral sentiment.
validation_note: 36 labeled past events, pre-event detection rate vs FinBERT

bullets: General method-of-moments framework for treating LLM-generated features as covariates, illustrated with news-based sentiment and stock returns; sample period and size not stated.

LLMs, not named, extract sentiment and classifications, and AI-Powered Inference corrects input-dependent errors with a small human-labeled calibration set and weights combining multiple model-prompt pairs.
AI-PI keeps conclusions stable across model and prompt choices where naive regressions vary, cutting confidence interval length to roughly half that of using human labels alone.
291,314 product launches posted to Product Hunt between January 2018 and April 2026, with each launch as the unit of observation.
An outcome-blind LLM ideation-exposure index, model not named, measures how much general-purpose LLMs can assist ideation per category, using ChatGPT's release as the timing shock.
After ChatGPT the distinctive share of launches fell from 25 to 16 percent, with larger similarity gains in more exposed categories and within both new and experienced teams.
